ListItem.Para: Spoiler actuator is deactivated.
Position of deactivated spoiler is verified before each departure.
Trailing edge variable camber (cruise flaps) function operates normally.
Hydraulic spoilers operate normally.
Flap position/skew sensors operate normally.
Slat position sensors operate normally.
Appropriate weight and center-of-gravity limits are observed.
Appropriate performance adjustments are applied.

Note.Para: Use Boeing Onboard Performance Tool (OPT) to determine take-off weight and/or landing weight as it affects aircraft performance.
SPOILERS advisory message may remain displayed until spoiler deactivation procedure is completed.

The FLAPS GROUND INHIBIT status message may be displayed on the ground if flaps are commanded to move. If flaps are extended, do not attempt flap retraction in any mode until spoiler deactivation procedure is completed.
CMCF fault reports must be used to determine the inoperative spoiler.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the inoperative spoiler actuator. Verify the position of the deactivated spoiler before each departure (AMM DDG 27-61-02).

ListItem.Para: Deactivate inoperative spoiler actuator in dispatch position:
Remove power from the inoperative spoiler:
For spoiler 4 inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker ELECT ACTR-SPLR 4 and lock with an INOP tag.
For spoiler 5 inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker ELECT ACTR-SPLR 5 and lock with an INOP tag.
For spoiler 10 inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker ELECT ACTR-SPLR 10 and lock with an INOP tag.
For spoiler 11 inoperative, using a flight deck multi-function display only, open circuit breaker ELECT ACTR-SPLR 11 and lock with an INOP tag.
Remove power from the remaining EMA spoilers:
For spoiler 4, open circuit breaker ELECT ACTR-SPLR 4 and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
For spoiler 5, open circuit breaker ELECT ACTR-SPLR 5 and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
For spoiler 10, open circuit breaker ELECT ACTR-SPLR 10 and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
For spoiler 11, open circuit breaker ELECT ACTR-SPLR 11 and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
Gain access to the inoperative spoiler EMA.
Disconnect, cap, and stow the center J1 connector on the linear variable differential transformer (LVDT) for the associated inoperative EMA.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added the new step E to provide additional procedure on how to examine the actuator or a shearout.

ListItem.Para: Determine whether the actuator drive train has a shearout by adding weight to the inoperative spoiler.

Note.rev:revHighlight: Added the new step E to provide additional procedure on how to examine the actuator or a shearout.
Note.Para: If the weight is not available, proceed to step F and install the locking collar.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added the new step E to provide additional procedure on how to examine the actuator or a shearout.

ListItem.Para: Add a 100 lbs (45 kg) load to the inoperative spoiler.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added the new step E to provide additional procedure on how to examine the actuator or a shearout.

ListItem.Para: Remove the motor brake cap to show the manual drive port in the actuator.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added the new step E to provide additional procedure on how to examine the actuator or a shearout.

ListItem.Para: Cycle the spoiler up and down three times.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added the new step E to provide additional procedure on how to examine the actuator or a shearout.

ListItem.Para: Use the manual drive port in the actuator to move the spoiler up and down.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added the new step E to provide additional procedure on how to examine the actuator or a shearout.

ListItem.Para: Operate the actuator through the manual drive with a torque of 30 in-lb (3 N-m) or less at a speed of 200 RPM or less.

Note.rev:revHighlight: Added the new step E to provide additional procedure on how to examine the actuator or a shearout.
Note.Para: A motor brake cap in reverse position or a 5/16 inch hex drive can be used as a tool to operate the actuator.

Para.rev:revHighlight: Added the new step E to provide additional procedure on how to examine the actuator or a shearout.

ListItem.Para: If the spoiler panel does not move, then a shearout occurred in the actuator drive train.
Lock the inoperative spoiler actuator.
Raise the spoiler:

For spoiler actuator failure not due to shearout, remove the three retaining screws of the motor/brake cap on the EMA actuator. Using less than 30 lb-in (3 N-m) torque at a rate less than 200 rpm, use the motor shaft drive port to extend the spoiler trailing edge up.

Note.Para: If unable to move the actuator and spoiler panel, then a shearout has likely occurred within the actuator gear train.

ListItem.Para: For spoiler actuator failure due to shearout, move the spoiler by hand.
Visually inspect the trailing edge flap in the vicinity of the inoperative spoiler to ensure no damage is present.
Lower the spoiler.

For spoiler actuator failure not due to shearout, lower the spoiler using EMA actuator. Using less than 30 lb-in (3 N-m) torque at a rate less than 200 rpm, use the motor shaft drive port to retract spoiler until the actuator cylinder is close to the required position. Measuring tool K27051 can be used to position the spoiler actuator.
For spoiler actuator failure due to shearout, move the spoiler by hand.
Lock actuator by installing the motor/brake cap in the reverse position and securing with the three retaining screws. Remove the measuring tool K27051 if used.
Install dispatch tool K27059 for spoiler actuator shearout, if there is a concern that the spoiler actuator may shear in a future flight, or if desired with no shearout:
Place dispatch tool clamp half on actuator and rotate 180 degrees into position.
Place and hold dispatch tool clamp half assembly in position.
Install nut and screw combination (3 places) and torque screws to 20 to 40 lb-in.
Ensure spoiler panel is locked in position.
Install lock wire through clamp and screw head (3 places).
Close circuit breakers for the operating EMA spoilers. Confirm the circuit breaker for the inoperative spoiler remains locked with an INOP tag.
Use the CMCF special function SPOILER DEACTIVATION CONFIRMATION to tell the Flight Control Electronics which spoiler you have deactivated with this procedure.
Confirm SPOILERS advisory message is not displayed and SPOILER ACTUATOR status message is displayed.
With hydraulics on, flaps retracted, and spoilers retracted, visually confirm the deactivated spoiler trailing edge is deflected upward approximately 1 inch relative to adjacent spoiler(s).
Verify the position of the deactivated spoiler before each departure.
With hydraulics on, flaps retracted, and spoilers retracted, visually confirm the deactivated spoiler is deflected upward approximately 1 inch relative to adjacent spoilers.

Note.Para: Speedbrakes can be used normally, but deployment with a spoiler deactivated will result in a roll and yaw asymmetry. When in the normal flight control mode, automatic rudder trim activity may be observed as the flight control system compensates for this asymmetry. Manual rudder trim inputs should not be made when in the normal mode of operation, unless directed by a non-normal checklist. A manual rudder trim input disables the automatic transfer of any steady state lateral control to the rudder and can cause increased drag and fuel consumption for the remainder of the flight.
The deactivated spoiler will be approximately 1 inch trailing edge up to preclude contact with trailing edge flaps. This spoiler will be shown on the Flight Control synoptic display as a blank box.

ListItem.Para: Limit Takeoff Weight Limit by:
Dispatch with takeoff and landing performance based on one spoiler inoperative using Boeing performance software.
Increase flight planning fuel by either using an airplane drag correction factor of 0.8% or increase the flight planning fuel by 1.0%.
Reduce performance limited weight with one spoiler actuator deactivated by:

Note.Para: The enroute climb weight penalties are based on single engine operating speeds that approximate maximum lift-to-drag ratio speed. To account for the difference in level-off altitude when operating at other speeds, multiply the enroute climb weight penalty listed above by the appropriate factor listed on page 3.00-01-00.2 in the ENROUTE DIVERSION SPEED EFFECTS paragraph.

ListItem.Para: Spoiler actuator is deactivated.
Position of deactivated spoiler is verified before each departure.
Trailing edge variable camber (cruise flaps) function operates normally.
Electro-mechanical (EMA) spoilers operate normally.
Flap position/skew sensors operate normally.
Slat position sensors operate normally.
Appropriate weight and center-of-gravity limits are observed.
Appropriate performance adjustments are applied.

Note.Para: Use Boeing Onboard Performance Tool (OPT) to determine take-off weight and/or landing weight as it affects aircraft performance.
For spoilers 6 and 9, if the spoiler fault is due to a failure of the associated Remote Electronics Unit (REU), the non-dispatchable FLAP INSPECTION status message will display after every landing.
The Wings Lock function of the Flight Control Surfaces Lock system will be inoperative after deactivation procedure is completed. The airplane must also be dispatched using MEL item 27-02-05.
SPOILERS advisory message may remain displayed until spoiler deactivation procedure is completed.

The FLAPS GROUND INHIBIT status message may be displayed on the ground if flaps are commanded to move. If flaps are extended, do not attempt flap retraction in any mode until spoiler deactivation procedure is completed.
CMCF fault reports must be used to determine the inoperative spoiler.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the inoperative spoiler actuator. Verify the position of the deactivated spoiler before each departure (AMM DDG 27-61-02).

ListItem.Para: Deactivate inoperative spoiler actuator:
Confirm hydraulic systems are off.
For hydraulic powered spoiler inoperative (spoilers 1, 2, 3, 6, 7, 8, 9, 12, 13, 14), deactivate spoiler actuator in dispatch position:
For spoiler 3 or 12 inoperative, open circuit breaker FCE CABINET-L BACKUP and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
For spoiler 1 or 14 inoperative, open circuit breaker FCE CABINET-C1 BACKUP and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
For spoiler 7 or 8 inoperative, open circuit breaker FCE CABINET-C2 BACKUP and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
For spoiler 2,6,9, or 13 inoperative, open circuit breaker FCE CABINET-R BACKUP and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
Gain access to the inoperative spoiler actuator and use the manual release cam to extend the spoiler trailing edge up.
Visually inspect the trailing edge flap in the vicinity of the inoperative spoiler to ensure no damage is present.
Disconnect, cap and stow the electrical connector associated with the inoperative spoiler actuator.
Install the dispatch tool K27011 on the rod end of the inoperative spoiler actuator.
Install lock wire to the jam nuts (2 places).
Close the opened FCE cabinet circuit breaker.
Use the CMCF special function SPOILER DEACTIVATION CONFIRMATION to tell the Flight Control Electronics which spoiler you have deactivated with this procedure.
Confirm SPOILERS advisory message is not displayed and SPOILER ACTUATOR status message is displayed.
For spoiler 3 or 12 inoperative, turn hydraulics on and verify spoiler panel fully retracts against collar:
Pressurize left system.
Depressurize left system.
For spoiler 1,7,8, or 14 inoperative, turn hydraulics on and verify spoiler panel fully retracts against collar:
Pressurize center system.
Depressurize center system.
For spoiler 2,6,9, or 13 inoperative, turn hydraulics on and verify spoiler panel fully retracts against collar:
Pressurize right system.
Depressurize right system.
With hydraulics on, flaps retracted, and spoilers retracted, visually confirm the deactivated spoiler trailing edge is deflected upward approximately 1 inch relative to adjacent spoiler(s).
Verify the position of the deactivated spoiler before each departure.
With hydraulics on, flaps retracted, and spoilers retracted, visually confirm the deactivated spoiler trailing edge is deflected upward approximately 1 inch relative to adjacent spoiler(s).
